meta.title: Sintetic Prediction AI
meta.description: Sintetic Prediction AI — multi-client workspace for planners and digital media teams. Creative testing, simulations and reports grounded in your brand context.

home.tagline: Strategic foresight for media & group planning
home.heroDesc: {brand} brings media planning, public affairs and brand strategy into one rehearsal space: upload briefs or coverage and explore how stories, risks and reactions may unfold across channels. The {agentScale} engine surfaces robust scenarios so teams can aim for the {optimalSolution} with evidence—not guesswork.
home.slogan: Rehearse the conversation. Then commit the plan.
home.systemReadyDesc: Upload decks, reports or unstructured context to initialise a simulation aligned with your planning cycle.
home.step01Desc: Seed extraction & memory injection & GraphRAG construction
home.step02Desc: Entity extraction & persona generation & Agent config injection
home.step03Desc: Dual-platform parallel sim & auto-parse requirements & temporal memory
home.step04Desc: ReportAgent interacts with the post-simulation environment via rich tools
home.step05Desc: Chat with any simulated individual & converse with ReportAgent
home.promptPlaceholder: // Describe your simulation or prediction requirement in natural language

step1.ontologyDesc: LLM analyzes document content and simulation requirements, extracts reality seeds, and auto-generates a suitable ontology structure
step1.graphRagDesc: Based on the generated ontology, documents are auto-chunked and sent to Zep to build a knowledge graph, extracting entities and relations, forming temporal memory and community summaries
step1.buildCompleteDesc: Graph build is complete. Proceed to the next step for simulation environment setup.

step2.simInstanceDesc: Create a new simulation instance and pull world parameter templates
step2.generateAgentPersonaDesc: Combine context to auto-extract entities and relations from the knowledge graph, initialize simulated individuals, and assign unique behaviors and memories based on reality seeds
step2.dualPlatformConfigDesc: LLM intelligently sets world time flow, recommendation algorithms, each individual's active hours, posting frequency, event triggers, and more based on requirements and reality seeds
step2.initialActivationDesc: Auto-generate initial activation events and hot topics based on narrative direction to guide the simulation world's initial state
step2.setupCompleteDesc: Simulation environment is ready. You can now start the simulation.
step2.roundsConfigDesc: MiroFish auto-plans to simulate {hours} real-world hours, each round representing {minutesPerRound} minutes of elapsed time
step2.customTip: For first-time runs, we strongly recommend switching to 'Custom Mode' to reduce rounds for a quick preview and lower error risk
step2.personaDimExperienceDesc: Complete behavioral trajectory in this event

step3.oldSimCleared: Old simulation logs cleared, restarting simulation
step3.preparingGoBack: Preparing to return to Step 2, closing simulation...
step3.closeFailed: Failed to close simulation environment, attempting force stop...

step4.panelKeyFacts: Latest key facts from temporal memory

step5.chatWithAgent: Chat with any individual in the world
step5.reportAgentDesc: A conversational version of the report generation agent with access to 4 professional tools and MiroFish's complete memory
step5.toolInsightForgeDesc: Aligns real-world seed data with simulation state, combining Global/Local Memory for cross-temporal deep attribution analysis
step5.toolPanoramaSearchDesc: Graph-based BFS algorithm that reconstructs event propagation paths, capturing the full topology of information flow
step5.toolQuickSearchDesc: GraphRAG-based instant query interface with optimized indexing for fast extraction of node attributes and discrete facts
step5.toolInterviewSubAgentDesc: Autonomous interviews that conduct parallel multi-round dialogues with simulated individuals, collecting unstructured opinion data and psychological states
step5.chatEmptyReportAgent: Chat with Report Agent to explore report content in depth
step5.chatEmptyAgent: Chat with simulated individuals to understand their perspectives
step5.surveyInputPlaceholder: Enter the question you want to ask all selected targets...
step5.selectAgentFirst: Please select a simulated individual first

graph.graphMemoryRealtime: GraphRAG short/long-term memory updating in real-time
graph.pendingContentHint: Some content is still processing. Consider refreshing the graph manually later.

history.title: Simulation History
history.replayHint: Step 3 'Run Simulation' and Step 5 'Deep Interaction' must be started during runtime and do not support history replay

api.dbNotExist: Database does not exist. The simulation may not have run yet.

api.reportGenerateStarted: Report generation task started. Query progress via /api/report/generate/status.

api.envRunning: Environment is running and ready for Interview commands

topnav.guideHint: How to load context for good predictions

guide.tipBody: Treat this guide as the briefing standard for every client you onboard. Predictions are only as good as the context behind them.
guide.heroTitle: How to load context for good predictions
guide.heroSub: What to upload, in which shape, and what NOT to upload — so creative tests, predictions and the knowledge graph give you specific answers, not generic ones.

s1.title: How the tool processes what you upload

s2.title: The 5 information categories that drive predictions

s5.title: Recommended structure for a new client

s6.title: What a good result looks like

manual.heroSub: Reading order and conventions for planners and digital media. Estimated read: 6 minutes.

help.heroSub: Move faster across the workspace. All shortcuts are case-insensitive and follow the macOS / Windows convention.

td.compareSub: Radar across the 4 scoring dimensions, plus total scores.

quickPrediction.title: Quick prediction
quickPrediction.subtitle: Grounded on this client's knowledge graph
quickPrediction.placeholder: Ask anything: "how risky is the claim 'cheaper' on Black Friday?"
quickPrediction.hint: Ask any branding or marketing question. The answer is grounded on facts from this client's graph and cites them.

action.contextHint: Briefs, studies, copy and metrics. Each upload becomes an episode the agent can cite.
action.testHint: Compare 2-8 creative variants for a defined audience. Results in minutes.
action.simHint: Stress-test campaign decisions with synthetic agents on Twitter / Reddit.

home2.heroSub: A per-client workspace for planners and digital media. Every action is born inside a brand: context, creative tests, simulations and actionable reports.
home2.emptyHint: Create your first client to start grouping work and context.

creativeTest.title: Creative Testing
creativeTest.subtitle: Compare creative variants by audience and channel before launch.
creativeTest.disabledDesc: Enable the feature flag in the backend to opt-in:
creativeTest.businessGoalPh: What decision do you need to make?
creativeTest.imageHint: PNG, JPG or WebP. Single image adds visual_composition + visual_legibility scores; 2+ images become a carousel and add visual_narrative_coherence.
creativeTest.carouselMeta: Carousel · {n} slides — narrative coherence will be scored.
creativeTest.videoHint: MP4, MOV or WebM. Backend extracts up to 8 frames + a Whisper transcript. Adds video_pacing and (when audio present) audio_message_alignment scores.
creativeTest.headline: Headline
creativeTest.requireClientDesc: Every creative test must belong to a client. Open this wizard from a client page.
creativeTest.runningSubtitle: You can stay on this screen — progress updates in real time. Leaving keeps the run alive in the background.

help.businessGoal: The decision your team needs to make in the next 1-2 weeks. One sentence is enough — e.g. 'pick the launch claim for Black Friday MX'.
help.scenario: Context that justifies running this test now: campaign window, competitive pressure, urgency, market signal.
help.audience: Who you want to convince. The clearer the audience, the sharper the scoring per variant.
help.variants: 2 to 8 creative options to compare. Same goal, different angles, claims or CTAs. Each must have a headline.
help.channels: Where each variant could run. Comma-separated: instagram, tiktok, youtube, etc.
help.successMetrics: How you'll measure success after launch. Comma-separated: CTR, add-to-cart rate, brand lift.
help.winner: The variant with the highest combined score across the four dimensions.
help.recommendation: Suggested action for the winning variant: activate, iterate or discard.
help.ranking: All variants sorted by total score. Use it to compare the gap between the top option and the rest.
help.scorecards: Detailed view of each variant's strengths and weaknesses across the four scoring dimensions.

scoreDim.message_clarity_score: How unambiguous and memorable the core message is. 0-100.
scoreDim.audience_fit_score: How well the variant resonates with the declared audience. 0-100.
scoreDim.conversion_intent_score: How strongly it pushes the audience toward the success metrics. 0-100.
scoreDim.brand_risk_score: Brand safety: higher is better. Penalises claims that may misfire, fatigue or trigger reputational risk.
scoreDim.visual_composition_score: Image-only: balance, focal hierarchy and brand coherence of the asset. 0-100.
scoreDim.visual_legibility_score: Image-only: legibility of headline/CTA over the visual at thumbnail and feed-scroll sizes. 0-100.
scoreDim.visual_narrative_coherence_score: Carousel/video: how well the slides or frames flow as a sequence (setup → tension → payoff). Penalises jumps, repetition and confusing order. 0-100.
scoreDim.video_pacing_score: Video-only: rhythm and edit cadence across the extracted frames. Penalises dead frames or overcuts. 0-100.
scoreDim.audio_message_alignment_score: Video-only (transcript present): how well the voiceover or on-screen audio reinforces the visual claim. 0-100.

risk.low: Brand safety is solid; no material concerns detected.
risk.medium: Some friction with brand or compliance signals. Worth a second review before launch.
risk.high: Material risk detected — claim, tone or context could damage brand. Iterate before activating.

reco.activate: Confidence is high enough to launch. Brief the channel team.
reco.iterate: Promising but not ready. Adjust copy/CTA and re-run before committing budget.
reco.discard: Drop this variant. The combined evidence does not support launching.

howToRead.title: How to read this report
howToRead.intro: Each variant is scored on 4 dimensions and gets a total score. The variant with the highest total wins, unless its brand safety penalty is too high. Use this guide to interpret each section.
howToRead.totalScore: We combine clarity + fit + intent and subtract brand risk. The result drives the ranking. A high clarity but high-risk variant can still rank below a more balanced one.

errors.noPendingFiles: No files staged. Go back to the home and try again.

ui.waitingOntologyHint: The graph build will start automatically when ontology is ready.
ui.ontologyApiDesc: Once docs are uploaded, the LLM analyzes content and proposes an ontology (entity types + relation types) suited to opinion simulation.
ui.graphApiDesc: Using the ontology, documents are chunked and the graph builder extracts entities and relations.

clients.listSubtitle: Each client groups its brand context, creative tests, simulations and reports.
clients.emptyState: No clients yet. Create one to start grouping work and context.
clients.disabledDesc: Set DATABASE_URL and apply migration 002_clients.sql, then refresh.
clients.graphPending: (no graph yet — wired in next iteration)
clients.scopedSoon: Scoped to this client in the next iterations.
clients.scopedHint: This tab will list the items linked to this client once the per-module scoping ships. The default filter, navigation and exports are already in place.

creativeTests.empty: No creative tests yet for this client. Start a new test to compare variants.

simulations.empty: No simulations yet for this client. Launch a new one from the button above.
simulations.requirementPh: Describe the scenario, audience and question to answer. e.g. 'Product X launch in MX City, compare reactions of 18-24 vs 25-34 audiences'.
simulations.dropzone: Drag files here or click to pick. PDF, MD, TXT, DOCX.

reports.empty: No reports yet for this client. Once a simulation finishes, its report will appear here.

clients.contextHelper: Brand identity, market positioning, voice and references the team can share.
clients.creativeTestsHelper: Run multivariant tests within the scope of this client.
clients.reportsHelper: Reports produced by simulations under this client.
clients.simulationsHelper: Simulations launched within the scope of this client.

fields.name: Client name
fields.descriptionPh: Short summary of the account, scope, focus.
fields.brandGuidelinesPh: Voice, tone, do/don't, claim restrictions, compliance flags.

graph.bootstrapTitle: This client has no knowledge graph yet
graph.bootstrapDesc: Bootstrap the graph to start ingesting briefs, studies, copy and metrics. The graph powers semantic search and is reused by Creative Test, Reports and Simulations.
graph.uploadHelper: Drop briefs, studies, decks or copy as files. Each one becomes a separate episode in the graph.
graph.openGuide: How to write a good brief
graph.dropzoneEmpty: Drag & drop files here, or click to choose. PDF, MD and TXT supported.
graph.addContextHelper: Paste a brief, a study, copy or any text that defines this client. Each upload becomes an episode in the graph.
graph.sourcePlaceholder: Source label (optional). e.g. 'BF24 brief', 'Q1 brand survey'.
graph.searchHelper: Ask anything across briefs, studies and copy already ingested for this client.
graph.searchPlaceholder: What did the Q1 study say about pricing?
graph.miniHint: Each colour is an entity type. Drag, zoom and click in full view to inspect any node and its neighbours.
graph.selectHint: Click any node to see its summary and neighbours. Drag nodes to reorganise.
